sections.paragraph:
plur. חֳרָבֹות, with art. הֶחֳרָבֹות const. חָרְבֹות f. [“

(1) dryness, pl. dry places, Isa 48:21.”]

(2) a desolation, a place laid waste, ruins. Lev 26:31, נָתַתִּי אֶת־עָרֵיכֶם חָרְבָּה “I will lay your cities waste.” בָּנָה תֳרָבֹות to build up ruins or places laid waste. Eze 36:10, 33 Eze 36:33 38:12 Mal 1:4 Isa 58:12 61:4 . Job 3:14, “kings and counsellors of the earth הַבֹּנִים חֳרָבֹות לָמֹו who have built ruins for themselves,” i.e. splendid edifices, presently however to fall into ruins, q.d. die große Steinhaufen aufbauen. Synonymous with this is הֵקִים חֳרָבֹות Isa 44:26. חָרְבֹות מֵחִים the ruins, i.e. the ruined houses of the rich, Isa 5:17.
